analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: The model runs in eval() mode, so every BatchNorm2d lowers to aten::_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training (inference-mode affine: y = gamma*(x-running_mean)/sqrt(running_var+eps) + beta). With frozen running statistics this is a per-channel affine transform whose scale and shift are compile-time constants. Inductor currently emits it as standalone DRAM-bound elementwise Triton kernels chained after each convolution rather than folding it into the conv. The BN+ReLU Triton kernels are saturated on memory bandwidth, not compute: they read the full activation tensor once and do almost no arithmetic, so they spend their time moving bytes. Folding the BN affine into the preceding convolution's weight/bias makes the BN math free (absorbed into weights at trace time) and removes the extra full-tensor read/write passes.

transformation.description: Eval-mode Conv-BatchNorm folding. Match the pattern (aten.convolution -> aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training) in the Aten IR. Because batch_norm is in no_training (inference) mode, gamma/beta/running_mean/running_var are constant tensors. Compute folded_weight = conv_weight * (gamma / sqrt(running_var + eps))[:, None, None, None] and folded_bias = beta - running_mean * gamma / sqrt(running_var + eps) (conv has bias=False here, so the folded bias is created fresh). Replace the conv node's weight input with the folded weight constant, give the conv a bias, and erase the batch_norm node by redirecting all its uses to the conv output. The trailing aten.relu then becomes a direct consumer of the conv and Inductor fuses it into the conv epilogue or a single pointwise kernel. This removes all five _native_batch_norm_legit_no_training_* elementwise passes (each a full-tensor DRAM round trip) at zero numerical cost.
transformation.location: Inductor pre-grad / joint-graph FX pass over the Aten IR, before Inductor lowering. Equivalent to torch.fx.experimental.optimization.fuse / nn.utils.fuse_conv_bn_weights applied at graph level.
fx_steps: for node in graph.find_nodes(op='call_function', target=torch.ops.aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default):
conv = node.args[0]  # the convolution producing BN input
if conv.op != 'call_function' or conv.target != torch.ops.aten.convolution.default: continue
if len(conv.users) != 1: continue  # conv output must feed only this BN
w = conv.args[1]; gamma = node.args[1]; beta = node.args[2]; running_mean = node.args[3]; running_var = node.args[4]; eps = node.args[7]
# materialize folded weight/bias as graph constants via get_attr or fold into the parameter at trace time
folded_w, folded_b = torch.nn.utils.fuse_conv_bn_weights(W, conv_bias_or_None, running_mean, running_var, eps, gamma, beta)
with graph.inserting_before(conv):
new_w = graph.create_node('get_attr', folded_w_name); new_b = graph.create_node('get_attr', folded_b_name)
conv.update_arg(1, new_w); conv.update_arg(2, new_b)  # arg index 2 is conv bias
node.replace_all_uses_with(conv)
graph.erase_node(node)
graph.lint(); graph.recompile()
transformation.code_hint: Register an Inductor pre-grad custom pass via torch._inductor.config.pre_grad_custom_pass, or use the built-in torch._inductor.fx_passes.binary_folding / efficient_conv_bn_eval path. The numerically equivalent helper is torch.nn.utils.fuse_conv_bn_weights.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes 5 full-activation-tensor read/write passes (the BN+ReLU elementwise kernels run at 70-85% DRAM throughput). Largest single saving is the 32x32x256 stage BN pass at ~27 us; the conv-epilogue ReLU that remains touches the data the conv already has resident.

optimizations.notes: Safe and standard for eval()/inference. Numerically exact (algebraic fold). Must run before any pass that registers folded weights as buffers and before layout changes (OPT-2). After folding, the per-stage BN+ReLU Triton kernels (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm_legit_no_training_relu_*) should disappear or collapse into the conv epilogue.

bottleneck.description: Every cudnn_convolution operator carries 2 satellite convertTensor_kernel launches. cuDNN selected an NHWC tensor-core kernel (cutlass_tensorop_s1688fprop_optimized_tf32_128x64_16x6_NHWC_align4), but the FX graph carries activations in the default NCHW (contiguous) layout. Inductor therefore inserts layout-conversion kernels (convertTensor_kernel / nchwToNhwcKernel) immediately before and after each conv to transpose NCHW<->NHWC. These conversions are pure data movement with no useful compute and add ~3-7 us per conversion, 2 conversions per conv stage. Keeping the entire conv stack in channels_last (NHWC) memory format makes the cuDNN NHWC kernel consume and produce its native layout directly, eliminating the convert kernels.

transformation.description: Propagate channels_last (NHWC) memory format through the conv stack so the cuDNN NHWC tensor-core kernel runs without surrounding layout conversions. At the model level this is model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) plus input.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last). As an FX pass it is implemented by inserting a single aten.clone with memory_format=torch.channels_last on the network input placeholder and letting the convolution layout-propagation rules carry NHWC through; the redundant per-conv aten ops that force a contiguous (NCHW) restride between convs are removed so no intermediate convertTensor is emitted. The trailing aten.mean (AdaptiveAvgPool) and the flatten before addmm restore the expected layout once at the end.
transformation.location: Inductor layout-optimization pass over the Aten IR (torch._inductor.config.layout_optimization), or model-level set_memory_format before compile. FX targets: the graph input placeholder and each torch.ops.aten.convolution.default node's stride/layout metadata.
fx_steps: placeholder = next(n for n in graph.nodes if n.op == 'placeholder')
with graph.inserting_after(placeholder):
cl = graph.call_function(torch.ops.aten.clone.default, args=(placeholder,), kwargs={'memory_format': torch.channels_last})
placeholder.replace_all_uses_with(cl); cl.update_arg(0, placeholder)
# remove intermediate restride-to-contiguous nodes between consecutive convolution nodes so NHWC propagates
for node in graph.find_nodes(op='call_function', target=torch.ops.aten.convolution.default):
# ensure no aten.clone/contiguous(memory_format=contiguous) sits on the conv input path
pass
graph.lint(); graph.recompile()
transformation.code_hint: torch._inductor.config.layout_optimization = True is usually on, but the NCHW input forces conversions; the robust fix is model = model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) and x = x.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) prior to torch.compile. Verify the convertTensor_kernel launches vanish in the recaptured trace.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates ~10 NCHW<->NHWC transpose passes (each a full-tensor read+write). The convs themselves are unchanged (already NHWC tensor-core) but no longer pay layout-shuffle overhead on either side.

optimizations.notes: Must run after OPT-1: BN folding rewrites conv weight/bias as new constants, and the layout pass must see the final (folded) conv nodes. The two cudnn convs that dominate the budget are already 66-73% tensor-core active at TF32, so the conv math itself is near its achievable efficiency on this Blackwell part; the realistic FX lever is removing the surrounding convert/BN overhead, not the GEMM cores. Occupancy is only 8.33% but that is inherent to the 150-register cutlass tile and is not addressable from FX.

global_notes: 2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 1.5% of attributed time): aten::addmm op_id=21 (3104 ns) and aten::addmm op_id=42 (3072 ns). The classifier GEMM (16x256x10) is a tiny gemmSN_TN cublas kernel with no ncu counters captured; not worth an FX pass.

Time budget computed from operators[].aggregated.total_duration_ns per protocol (sum = 409219 ns = 0.409 ms). This differs from the ~1.076 ms wall-time figure in the task brief because (a) duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and used only for relative ranking, and (b) the prologue's aggregated block counts 14 unique deduplicated kernels while its raw kernels[] array lists ~55 launches across measurement iterations.

fused_kernel_double_count flag: layer::unique::prologue (is_fused=true) is the deduplicated representative of the entire forward pass and re-attributes the same underlying cudnn/triton kernels that also appear under the separate aten::cudnn_convolution and aten::addmm operators. The prologue and the per-aten conv operators are NOT additive; OPT-1 and OPT-2 target the same physical kernels viewed through both lenses. Impact estimates are deduplicated to the physical-kernel level.

warm_up_inflation flag: capture_metadata has no measure_iters field, and the prologue kernels[] array contains repeated kernel names (two forward iterations visible), so per-kernel launch counts may include more than one measured iteration. Relative ranking is unaffected.

Blackwell sm_120: warp_cycles_per_instruction is null in all aggregated blocks (counter removed on Blackwell). Latency-bound assessment uses eligible_cycles_pct: the conv kernels sit at ~26% eligible cycles and the BN/pool kernels at ~3-22%, consistent with the BN/pool kernels being DRAM-bandwidth bound and the convs being tensor-core/compute bound.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (Conv+BN fold) -> OPT-2 (channels_last layout). OPT-2 must see the folded conv nodes; running layout propagation before folding would act on conv weights that are about to be rewritten. No cycles.

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were not available; FX API patterns are drawn from standard torch.fx / torch._inductor surgery APIs, and knowledge/fx-patterns.md + knowledge/hardware-limits.md were not present in the repo. Per fallback policy this caps novel transforms at medium confidence, which is why OPT-2 is medium; OPT-1 remains high because eval-mode conv-BN folding is a well-established, numerically exact transformation.
